session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9227 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll follow the skill instructions and fetch the PR feedback for PR 9227:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: TRUNK=$(erk exec detect-trunk-branch | jq -r '.trunk_branch') && git diff --stat -M -C "$TRUNK"...HEAD
Fetch file restructuring context with trunk branch

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 9227, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9227", "pr_title": "Make `erk pr address` launch Claude interactively", "review_threads": [], "discussion_comments": [ { "id": 4049434006, "author": "schrockn", "body": "🚀 Starting implementation --- **Worktree:** `erk-slot-26` **Branch:** `plnd/address-interactive-launch-03-12-1405`", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9227#issuecomment-4049434006" }, { "id": 4049450114, "author": "schrockn", "body": "* **#9227** 👈 (View in Graphite) * `master`

This stack of pull requests is managed by Graphite. Learn more about stacking. Inline comments posted for each.
Details ### Patterns Checked ✅ LBYL over EAFP - None found ✅ Exception handling - None found ✅ Path operations - None found ✅ Import organization - None found ✅ No default parameters (production) - None found ✅ Frozen dataclasses - None found ❌ Nested ternaries - Found in src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py:81 ### Violations Summary - `address_cmd.py:81`: Nested ternary expression with multiple lines (outer ternary wrapping inner ternary) ### Files Reviewed - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py`: 1 violation - `tests/commands/pr/test_address.py`: 0 violations --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-12 13:17:43 PT**: Found 1 nested ternary violation in address_cmd.py refactor - **2026-03-12 12:29:16 PT**: No violations detected in address_cmd refactor and test simplification
", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9227#issuecomment-4049456817" }, { "id": 4049457465, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": " ## ✅ Test Coverage Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-12 13:17:32 PT No violations found. All source code changes have corresponding test coverage.
Details ### Files Reviewed | File | Status | Test Coverage | |------|--------|---| | `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py` | Modified | ✅ `tests/commands/pr/test_address.py` | ### Test Changes Summary - **Tests refactored**: Command handler tests restructured to use simpler fake objects (`FakeAgentLauncher`) - **Net test reduction**: 8 old tests → 7 new tests (coverage refactored, not reduced) - **Coverage maintained**: All key scenarios tested (flag handling, mode selection, error handling, live_dangerously config) ### Patterns Checked ✅ All source modifications have test coverage ✅ No untestable files found ✅ No subprocess/security violations detected --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-12 13:17:32 PT**: All source changes covered by tests; test refactoring simplified test suite while maintaining coverage
", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9227#issuecomment-4049457465" ... [15 lines omitted] ... "body": " ## ✅ Tripwires Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-12 13:18:00 PT No tripwire violations detected. Clean refactoring following erk best practices.
Details ### Tripwires Triggered - CLI error handling patterns → loaded cli/tripwires.md - Testing patterns → loaded testing/tripwires.md ### Tier 1 Pattern Matches ✅ `Path\.home\(\)` - None found ✅ `@patch|monkeypatch\.` - None found ✅ `monkeypatch\.setattr.*Path\.home|monkeypatch.*subprocess\.run` - None found ✅ `monkeypatch\.chdir\(\)` - None found ### Tier 2 Semantic Matches ✅ Gateway pattern usage - Correctly uses `ctx.agent_launcher` ✅ Fake-based testing - Uses `FakeAgentLauncher` properly ✅ Test isolation - Uses `context_for_test()` pattern ✅ No backwards compatibility hacks - Clean refactoring ✅ No EAFP patterns - No try/except for control flow ✅ Frozen dataclass patterns - Proper usage with `InteractiveAgentConfig` ### Violations Summary None ### Files Reviewed - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/address_cmd.py`: 0 violations - `tests/commands/pr/test_address.py`: 0 violations --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-12 13:18:00 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Clean refactoring following erk best practices. - **2026-03-12 12:29:44 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Clean refactoring following erk best practices.

Now I'll classify the feedback according to the skill instructions:
Based on the PR feedback data, I can classify the comments:
1. **schrockn implementation start** - Informational (Graphite stack context) 2. **Graphite stack comment** - Informational (Graphite automation comment) 3. **github-actions[bot] Dignified Python Review** - Actionable (1 nested ternary violation found) 4. **github-actions[bot] Test Coverage Review** - Informational (no violations, all changes covered) 5. **github-actions[bot] Dignified Code Simplifier Review** - Actionable (same nested ternary violation) 6. **github-actions[bot] Tripwires Review** - Informational (no violations detected)
The key findings: - No file restructuring (standard modifications) - 2 actionable bot comments about the same nested ternary operator issue - Multiple informational comments (status updates, coverage confirmation)
